Factory SOPs
RecipeSOP CodeBrandVersionStepsStatus
Factory Standard Operating Procedure
Hasawi Marinade
SOP-2025-009 · Based on Recipe v2.1 · Issued 15 Mar 2025
Approved for Production
Brand
Maestro
Batch Size
Per run
Scale Factor
×N
Storage
Frozen −18°C
Shelf Life
12 Months
Production Steps8 Steps
1
🧼 Hygiene🌡️ Temperature
Equipment Preparation & Sanitation
Ensure all mixing vessels, pipelines and filling equipment are CIP-cleaned and sanitised per SAN-001 protocol before commencing production. Verify equipment calibration logs are up to date.
CIP Temperature
85°C minimum
Hold Time
20 minutes
Responsible
Sanitation Lead
2
⚖️ Weigh✅ QC Check
Raw Material Weighing & Verification
Weigh all dry ingredients on calibrated floor scales. Each ingredient must be verified against the batch ticket by two operators (4-eyes check). Record actual weights in production log.
Total Batch
400.000 kg
Scale Tolerance
±0.050 kg
Verification
4-eyes check
🛑 CCP 1 — Correct ingredient weights are a Critical Control Point. Any deviation >0.5% requires supervisor sign-off before proceeding.
3
⏱️ Time
Dry Blend — Spice & Seasoning Mix
Add all dry spices and seasoning ingredients to the ribbon blender in the order specified on the batch ticket. Blend until homogeneous. Sieve through 2mm mesh before transfer.
Blender Speed
Medium — 45 RPM
Blend Time
8 minutes
Mesh Size
2 mm
4
🌡️ Temperature⚠️ Danger
Base Liquid Preparation
Combine water and vinegar in the jacketed mixing vessel. Heat to 60°C while stirring at low speed. Add salt and dissolve completely. Verify pH before proceeding.
Target Temp
60°C
Stirrer Speed
Low — 20 RPM
Target pH
3.8 – 4.4
🛑 CCP 2 — pH must be verified in-line before adding dry blend. Log result on QA checkpoint sheet QA-F-002.
5
💡 Tip⏱️ Time
Incorporation of Dry Blend into Base
Slowly add the dry blend to the liquid base while mixing at medium speed. Ensure no clumping — if lumps observed, increase speed briefly then return to medium. Add oil phase last.
Addition Rate
Slow — over 5 min
Mixer Speed
Medium — 35 RPM
Mix Time
15 minutes total
6
✅ QC Check⚠️ Danger
In-Process QC Check
QA technician takes sample for pH, Brix and viscosity checks. Product must be within spec before proceeding to filling. Organoleptic check by QA and NPD sign-off required.
pH Target
3.8 – 4.4
Brix Target
18 – 22 °Bx
Viscosity
800–1200 cP
⚠️ If any parameter is out of spec, HOLD product and notify QA Manager and NPD immediately. Do not proceed to filling.
7
⚖️ Weigh📸 Photo Check
Filling & Packaging
Fill into designated packaging format per pack spec. Check fill weights every 15 minutes. Apply batch code and best-before date label. Confirm label compliance before pallet build.
Fill Weight
Per pack spec
Weight Check
Every 15 min
Label Check
Every pallet
8
🌡️ Temperature⚠️ Danger
Blast Freezing & Storage
Transfer filled and sealed product to blast freezer immediately after packing. Freeze to core temperature of −18°C before palletising. Store in frozen warehouse at −18°C ±2°C.
Blast Freeze
−35°C / 90 min
Core Temp Target
−18°C
Storage
−18°C ±2°C
🛑 CCP 3 — Core temperature must reach −18°C before product leaves blast freezer. Record on HACCP log HA-F-008.

TOTAL 372.400

Sign-off

Recipe Lifecycle

How a recipe moves from idea to production

📝
Draft
R&D creates recipe, adds ingredients, method
DRAFT
R&D
📤
In Review
Team reviews recipe, costing, formulation
IN REVIEW
R&D
🏭
Factory Trial
First factory batch. Factory runs, QA tests micro & physical. Factory records indicative yield.
FACTORY TRIAL
QA ✓
+ Recipe ID
🔬
Prod Trial
Full-scale factory batch. QA validates again, sensory panel evaluates. Factory records validated yield.
PROD TRIAL
QA ✓
Data ✓
✅
Approved
Production ready. R&D signs off when all data is complete
APPROVED
🧪
R&D
Creates & edits recipes
Moves recipe through stages
Final approval authority
Manages costing & formulation
🏭
Factory
Schedules production dates
Runs trial batches
Records yield & waste
Marks production complete
🔬
QA
Tests micro & physical results
Signs off trial stages
Gatekeeper for next stage
Manages allergens & nutrition
Production Run Lifecycle
⏳
Pending
Auto-created when R&D sends to trial. No date yet.
PENDING
Factory sets date
📋
Scheduled
Date is set. Factory knows when to run the batch.
SCHEDULED
Factory completes
✅
Completed
Yield & waste recorded. Completion date stamped.
COMPLETED
Gates at Each Stage What's required for each transition
Draft → In Review
🧪R&D moves it · no other gates
In Review → Factory Trial
🧪R&D moves it
📅Auto-action: Factory Trial run is scheduled
Factory Trial → Prod Trial
🔬QA signs off Factory Trial micro + physical
🏭Factory assigns Recipe ID (e.g. 211_BCS v1)
📊Factory records Indicative yield (soft step — not blocking)
📅Auto-action: Prod Trial run is scheduled
Prod Trial → Approved
🔬QA signs off Prod Trial micro + physical
💰Costing complete (cost/kg calculated)
⚠️Allergens declared
📊Nutrition data filled in
📈Factory records Validated yield (soft step — cost math falls back to indicative if missing)
🔐 Role gate: only R&D and Admin can move a recipe between stages. Only Factory or Admin can assign the Recipe ID. QA sign-off must be entered from the QA page.
ℹ️ Sensory evaluation is tracked outside RecipeHub. R&D confirms sensory results are acceptable before approving.
Code System

How Codes Work in the Factory

Two codes track every product — the NPD code (R&D) and the Recipe ID (Factory)

NPD Code → Recipe ID Lifecycle Which code is active at each stage
Stage Who owns it Code in use Notes
1 · NPD Draft NPD team 2026-220 · NPD code only Recipe is a concept, not yet producible
2 · R&D Sign-off NPD + QA 2026-220 · NPD code Ingredients validated, costing estimate done
3 · Factory Trial Production 2026-220 · NPD code still Factory runs first trial, collects feedback
4 · Trial Passed Factory assigns 2026-220 + 211_BCS v1 Factory adds the Recipe ID here, before R&D moves the recipe to Prod Trial
5 · Active Production Both 2026-220 + 211_BCS v1 Recipe ID is operational; NPD kept as historical tracker (recall, audit)
Factory Codification 415 active codes · reference
Recipes — 153 codes
211_* · Meat Factory line
100 recipes
212_* · Bakery line
53 recipes
Ingredients — 262 codes, grouped by prefix
RM — Raw Materials 184 codes
RMADT additives, ambient · 38
RMSET spices, dry · 29
RMTOC tomato/veg, chilled · 29
RMSAT sauces, ambient · 18
RMDPC dairy, chilled · 10
RMTOT tomato/veg, ambient · 9
RMMPF meat/protein, frozen · 9
RMGPT grain/pasta, ambient · 8
RMGPA grain artisanal · 7
RMTOF tomato/veg, frozen · 5
RMMPC meat/protein, chilled · 5
RMOLT oil, ambient · 3
RMSEA spice, ambient whole · 3
RMADA/C/F additive variants · 4
Other singletons RMBRT, RMDPT, RMDST, RMBVC, RMSAA, RMSEC, RMGPF · 7
PM — Packaging 42 codes
PMPBA plastic bags · 15
PMLAA labels · 12
PMPFA film/laminate rolls · 10
PMOSA cups, misc · 3
PMPXA boxes · 2
SF — Semi-Finished 27 codes
SFTOC vegetable WIPs · 18
SFSAC sauce WIPs chilled · 3
SFSAF sauce WIPs frozen · 3
SFSAT sauce WIPs ambient · 1
SFCRF / SFTOF crust/veg frozen · 2
CO — Consumables / Operational 5 codes
SCCRF — Semi-Finished Crust 4 codes
Grand Total 415 codes
💡 How to read a code: first 2 letters = material family (RM, PM, SF, CO, SC). Letters 3–4 = sub-category. Last letter = storage state (A ambient · C chilled · F frozen · T ambient tray · etc).
Factory SOP Anatomy

How a Factory SOP is Assembled

Three layers combine per recipe — shared blocks (library) + recipe-specific steps + kitchen method

Layer 1
Pre-Production — Shared Blocks
Generic steps reused across recipes — receiving, sifting, weighing, line clearance. Managed centrally in the Edit Library (admin-only). Each recipe toggles relevant blocks on/off; editing a block updates it everywhere.
11 blocks · numbered 3.0 – 3.9
Layer 2
Production Steps — Recipe-Specific
Bespoke steps that belong only to this recipe. Carries icons (🌡️ temp · ⚖️ weigh · ⏱️ time · 🧼 hygiene · ⚠️ danger · ✅ check · 📸 photo · 💡 tip), free-text params (label/value), CCP badges, and warning banners.
Distilled from the recipe's kitchen method; factory adds parameters + photos over time
Layer 3
Post-Production — Shared Blocks
Cooling, blast freezing, packaging, metal detection, FG storage, QA release, dispatch, transport. Same toggle pattern as pre-prod; editing central definition flows through.
11 blocks · numbered 3.10 – 3.20
📎
Annexures
Each block and step references forms (e.g. DFC-QA-F035 Material Receiving & Initial Inspection Records). The Factory SOP automatically collects every referenced form into a numbered Annexures table at the bottom — no manual listing.
ℹ️
Kitchen method vs Factory SOP
The recipe method (shown on the Recipe detail) is the kitchen/trial instructions — free-form prose. The Factory SOP is the production-floor document: the three layers above, scaled and formal. R&D drafts the method; Factory refines the SOP steps; the shared blocks are maintained by admin.
Product Builds & Branch SOPs

Product Build Lifecycle

How a product build goes from creation to branch rollout

🍕
Create Build
R&D defines product — components, assembly, portions, costing
R&D
💰
Cost & Comply
Costing, allergens, nutrition calculated from components
R&D QA
✅
Active
Build is live. Can be linked to Branch SOPs for rollout
ACTIVE
⛔
Discontinued
Product removed from menu. Linked Branch SOPs show warning
DISCONTINUED
Branch SOP Lifecycle
📋
Draft
Create SOP from a build. Add step photos, portions, instructions
DRAFT
R&D reviews
👁️
In Review
Team checks accuracy of photos, steps, and portions
IN REVIEW
Approved
✅
Approved
Printed and sent to branches. Allergens & nutrition pulled from linked build
APPROVED
🔗 Branch SOPs are linked to Product Builds. Allergens, nutrition, and costing are inherited automatically. If the build is discontinued, the SOP shows a warning banner.

How to Use the App
⚙
Factory SOP
1. Open recipe → Generate Factory SOP
2. Steps pulled from recipe method
3. Add photos to each step
4. Print: Full (photos) or Text Only
5. Regenerate anytime from More ⋯ menu
📋
Branch SOP
1. Create from a Build or + New
2. Upload step photos
3. Use ▲ ▼ to reorder steps
4. Print colour-coded cards for stores
🍕
Product Builds
1. Add components by type (Dough, Sauce, Cheese, Protein, Topping, Finish, Packaging, Bread, Seasoning)
2. Set Selling Price → see COGS %
3. Each size = its own build
4. Export PDF build sheet
✅
QA Sign-off
1. Open a recipe in Factory/Prod Trial
2. Sign off the trial (QA decides)
3. Set shelf life during sign-off
4. Upload lab certificates
🥕
Ingredients DB
1. Browse all ingredients with costs
2. Click Used in to find every recipe & build using that ingredient
3. Costs auto-sync from EBS
📅
Production Plan
1. See sent date & days waiting
2. Schedule with date change tracking
3. Add comments per production run
4. Edit batch size before scheduling
✉
Communications
1. Send messages by role or to everyone
2. Available to all roles except Viewers
3. Full message log with history
📁
Library
1. Upload documents (Admin + R&D only)
2. Everyone can view & download
3. Organised by category
📝
Import & Reorder Steps
1. Paste multiple method steps at once
2. Use ▲ ▼ arrows to reorder
3. Works in recipe edit & Branch SOP edit
🔄
Refresh Data
Pull latest from server
💾
Save All Data
Push your changes
📷
Photos
Click to zoom, upload to recipes/builds/SOPs
💬
Comments
On recipes & production runs
🔍
Used In
Find recipes using an ingredient
👤
Profile
Click your name in sidebar
🔒
Per-Item Saves
Recipes, builds, and SOPs save individually so multiple people can work at the same time without overwriting each other.

3. Each size = its own build

4. Everyone can view + download
